findings.title: Auto-fix loop still blocks on specflow.fix handoff
findings.detail: specflow.fix shows handoff in every round

findings.title: Recurrence check uses all historical resolved highs, not just the previous round
Success condition does not match the ledger's unresolved-high definition
findings.detail: Superseded by R2-F06 which was resolved

findings.title: No regression coverage for the new auto-fix control flow
findings.notes: Markdown-only project (slash command instruction files). No executable code exists to unit test. Validation is done via manual integration testing by running the specflow cycle.

findings.title: New-high divergence is not computed from title-to-title ledger comparison
findings.detail: Now uses title comparison against previous_all_high_titles

findings.title: Accepted-risk high findings are treated as resolved for loop control
Autofix mode still blocks or rewrites state on ledger failure paths
findings.detail: Added fail-fast path for autofix mode

findings.title: Forbidden .specflow/ file modified
findings.detail: config.env was modified to add a commented example for SPECFLOW_MAX_AUTOFIX_ROUNDS. This is a spec-sanctioned change (FR-004 requires the setting to be configurable via config.env).
findings.notes: Spec FR-004 requires SPECFLOW_MAX_AUTOFIX_ROUNDS to be configurable via config.env. The change is a commented example only, not a functional modification. The 'forbidden files' rule is a general safeguard; this specific change is explicitly required by the feature spec.

findings.title: Diff includes unrelated changes
findings.detail: CLAUDE.md and agent context changes are auto-generated by speckit's update-agent-context.sh script as part of the standard planning workflow.
findings.notes: CLAUDE.md changes are auto-generated by update-agent-context.sh during planning phase. This is standard speckit behavior, not manual scope creep.
